Petition For An Order Determining Interest In Community Property And Directing Trustee To Transfer Community Property Portion To The Petitioner
Off Calendar Jun-01-2026 Continued To Jul-13-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Hanlen Chang, Esq. appearing representing James A. Kushner, Petitioner and Surviving Spouse.
James Lamping, Esq. appearing representing James Abbenhuys, Objector and Trustor's Son.
Frank Cialone, Esq. appearing representing Carol Jensen, Trustee.

COURT REPORTER: Mary Ann Scanlen, CSR #8875; 415-834-1114/ma@scanlanstone.com

James Abbenhuys objects to petition for an order approving severed settlement agreement.

Objections to be filed by Jul-06-2026 or the Court will deem them waived.
Hearing is continued to Jul-13-2026 at 09:00 a.m. in Department 204 for the following:

A. Amended Petition for an Order Determining Interest in Community Property and Directing Trustee to transfer Community Property Portion to the Petitioner
B. Petition for an Order Approving Severed Settlement Agreement

Judge: Michael McNaughton; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Court Reporter: Mary Ann Scanlen, CSR #8875; 415-834-1114/ma@scanlanstone.com (D204)

MINI MINUTES: Hanlen Chang, Esq. appearing representing James A. Kushner, Petitioner and Surviving Spouse.
James Lamping, Esq. appearing representing James Abbenhuys, Objector and Trustor's Son.
Frank Cialone, Esq. appearing representing Carol Jensen, Trustee.

COURT REPORTER: Mary Ann Scanlen, CSR #8875; 415-834-1114/ma@scanlanstone.com

James Abbenhuys objects to petition for an order approving severed settlement agreement.

Objections to be filed by Jul-06-2026 or the Court will deem them waived.
Hearing is continued to Jul-13-2026 at 09:00 a.m. in Department 204 for the following:

A. Amended Petition for an Order Determining Interest in Community Property and Directing Trustee to transfer Community Property Portion to the Petitioner
B. Petition for an Order Approving Severed Settlement Agreement

Judge: Michael McNaughton; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Court Reporter: Mary Ann Scanlen, CSR #8875; 415-834-1114/ma@scanlanstone.com (D204)

Petition For Order Approving Settlement Agreement; For Modification And Termination Of Trust; And Related Relief
Off Calendar Mar-02-2026 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Hanlen J. Chang for surviving spouse James A. Kushner (via Zoom).
James P. Lamping for petitioner James Abbenhuys (via Zoom).
Frank A. Cialone for trustee Carol Jensen.

The matter was called for hearing on 1) Amended Petition for an Order Determining Interest in Community Property and Directing Trustee to Transfer Community Property Portion to the Petitioner; and 2) Petition for Order Approving Settlement Agreement, for Modification and Termination of Trust.

The Court heard argument on the petition to approve the settlement agreement. Mr. Lamping argued the trustee waived objection under the Court's prior Breslin order and that the settlement agreement should be approved. Mr. Cialone argued the settlement sought to terminate the trust and remove the trustee without a properly noticed petition and that the trustee did not agree.

The Court denied the petition to approve the settlement agreement in its entirety. The Court found the trustee participated in mediation through counsel and that the proposed agreement went beyond the matters raised in the pleadings.

As to the severability provision relating to Mr. Kushner's community property claim, the Court declined to rule at this time and directed counsel to meet and confer. The Court stated that if no agreement is reached, a petition to enforce the severable portion must be filed and properly noticed. The Court authorized counsel to set the petition, if filed, for June 1, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

At Mr. Lamping's request, the Court ordered the trustee to provide a Probate Code-compliant accounting to the parties within 90 days.

The Court continued the matter to June 1, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Michael B. McNaughton; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Court reporter: Angela Pourtabib, CSR#13714, apourtabib@gmail.com, 415-601-7054 (D204)

Petition For An Order Determining Interest In Community Property And Directing Trustee To Transfer Community Property Portion To The Petitioner
Off Calendar Mar-02-2026 Continued To Jun-01-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Off Calendar Dec-22-2025 Continued To Mar-02-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For An Order Determining Interest In Community Property And Directing Trustee To Transfer Community Property Portion To The Petitioner
Off Calendar Nov-03-2025 Continued To Dec-22-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For An Order Determining Interest In Community Property And Directing Trustee To Transfer Community Property Portion To The Petitioner
Off Calendar Jun-30-2025 Continued To Nov-03-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Hanlen J. Chang appeared via zoom for James A. Kushner, (Petitioner).
James Lamping appeared via zoom for James Abbenhuys (respondent).

Mr. Lamping from the outset of the hearing requested a Breslin order, that mediation would cost $12,000.00, that each party could pay a third of the cost, and that it should be payable by the trustee.

The Court shall issue a Breslin Order in this matter and consider who shall pay. The mediation is to be completed by October 17, 2025. Parties to contact the examiner if the matter settles.

The matter is continued to November 3, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: A. Datt. Not reported.
